library_preparation_preflight.summary: Meaningful coverage of arquillian-test-impl-base normally needs the Arquillian core implementation at test runtime; the target artifact only brings core API/SPI transitively, while ManagerBuilder.create() reflectively instantiates org.jboss.arquillian.core.impl.ManagerImpl from arquillian-core-impl-base.

deterministic_setup.reason: The library's main lifecycle adapter and extension/context behavior are exercised through Arquillian Manager/ManagerBuilder; ManagerImpl is not in the target artifact's transitive compile dependencies but is required for normal in-memory Arquillian lifecycle tests.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property appears necessary. Generated tests should prefer in-memory Arquillian lifecycle coverage: build a Manager with ManagerBuilder, register the test extension/context handlers, and exercise EventTestRunnerAdaptor, TestContextHandler, TestInstanceEnricher, and ArquillianResourceTestEnricher with small local test classes and stub ServiceLoader/ResourceProvider implementations.
risks: Without arquillian-core-impl-base, tests may compile against ManagerBuilder but fail at runtime when ManagerImpl is reflectively loaded, or fall back to superficial direct-constructor coverage.
Upstream tests also used Mockito and test-helper classes, but equivalent stubs can be written in generated tests, so those are not deterministic setup requirements.
